questions.question: A sum of money at simple interest amounts to Rs. 815 in 3 years and to Rs. 854 in 4 years. The sum is:
questions.explanation: Simple Interest for 1 year = 854 - 815 = Rs. 39. Simple Interest for 3 years = 39 * 3 = Rs. 117. Principal = 815 - 117 = Rs. 698.

questions.question: The cost price of 20 articles is the same as the selling price of x articles. If the profit is 25%, then the value of x is:
questions.explanation: Let CP of each article be Re. 1. CP of x articles = Rs. x. SP of x articles = Rs. 20. Profit = 20 - x. (20-x)/x * 100 = 25. x = 16.

questions.question: In a 100 m race, A can give B 10 m and C 28 m. In the same race A can give B:
questions.explanation: When A covers 100m, B covers 90m and C covers 72m. When B covers 90m, C covers 72m. When B covers 100m, C covers (72/90)*100 = 80m. B can give C 20m.

questions.question: Two trains running in opposite directions cross a man standing on the platform in 27 seconds and 17 seconds respectively and they cross each other in 23 seconds. The ratio of their speeds is:
questions.explanation: Let speeds be x and y. Lengths are 27x and 17y. (27x+17y)/(x+y) = 23. 27x+17y = 23x+23y. 4x = 6y. x/y = 3/2.

questions.question: A bag contains 2 red, 3 green and 2 blue balls. Two balls are drawn at random. What is the probability that none of the balls drawn is blue?
questions.explanation: Total balls = 7. Ways to draw 2 balls = 7C2 = 21. Non-blue balls = 5. Ways to draw 2 non-blue = 5C2 = 10. Probability = 10/21.

questions.question: The average of 20 numbers is zero. Of them, at the most, how many may be greater than zero?
questions.explanation: To keep the average zero, the sum must be zero. We can have 19 large positive numbers and one large negative number balancing them.

questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 4 hours; B and C together can do it in 3 hours, while A and C together can do it in 2 hours. How long will B alone take to do it?
If 20% of a = b, then b% of 20 is the same as:
In how many different ways can the letters of the word 'LEADING' be arranged in such a way that the vowels always come together?
questions.explanation: Vowels are E, A, I. Consonants are L, D, N, G. Treat (EAI) as one unit. We have 5 units. They arrange in 5! = 120 ways. Vowels arrange in 3! = 6 ways. Total = 120 * 6 = 720.

questions.question: The ratio of the ages of A and B is 4:3. The ratio of their ages 6 years hence will be 5:4. Find their present ages.
A boat can travel with a speed of 13 km/hr in still water. If the speed of the stream is 4 km/hr, find the time taken by the boat to go 68 km downstream.
questions.explanation: Downstream speed = 13 + 4 = 17 km/hr. Time = 68/17 = 4 hours.

questions.question: A mixture of 150 liters of wine and water contains 20% water. How much more water should be added so that water becomes 25% of the new mixture?
questions.explanation: Wine = 80% of 150 = 120 liters. Water = 30 liters. In new mixture, wine is 75%. 75% of new total = 120. New total = 160. So, water added = 10 liters.

questions.question: Pipes A and B can fill a tank in 5 and 6 hours respectively. Pipe C can empty it in 12 hours. If all three pipes are opened together, the tank will be filled in:
questions.explanation: Net part filled in 1 hr = 1/5 + 1/6 - 1/12 = (12+10-5)/60 = 17/60. Tank filled in 60/17 hours.

questions.question: Out of 7 consonants and 4 vowels, how many words of 3 consonants and 2 vowels can be formed?
questions.explanation: Selection ways = 7C3 * 4C2 = 35 * 6 = 210. Arrangement of 5 letters = 5! = 120. Total words = 210 * 120 = 25200.

questions.question: At what rate of compound interest per annum will a sum of Rs. 1200 become Rs. 1348.32 in 2 years?
Choose the most appropriate synonym for 'EPHEMERAL':
questions.explanation: Ephemeral means lasting for a very short time, which is synonymous with transitory.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The manager was _______ by the employee's dedication.
questions.explanation: The passive voice construction requires the past participle 'impressed'.

questions.question: Choose the correct antonym for 'BENIGN':
questions.explanation: Benign means gentle or harmless. Malignant is the direct opposite, meaning harmful or malevolent.

questions.question: Identify the grammatical error in the sentence: 'Neither of the boys have returned yet.'
questions.explanation: 'Neither' takes a singular verb. It should be 'Neither of the boys has returned yet.'

questions.question: Select the correct passive form: 'They are building a new hospital.'
options: A new hospital is built by them.
A new hospital was being built by them.
A new hospital is being built by them.
A new hospital has been built by them.
questions.correctAnswer: A new hospital is being built by them.
questions.explanation: The active sentence is in present continuous tense. The passive equivalent is 'is/are being + past participle'.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the idiom 'To spill the beans'?
questions.explanation: 'To spill the beans' means to disclose a secret or reveal confidential information.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with appropriate preposition: 'He is completely absorbed _______ his work.'
questions.explanation: The correct preposition to follow 'absorbed' in this context is 'in'.

questions.question: Find the word with correct spelling:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling has double 'c' and double 'm' (Accommodation).

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words: 'A person who does not believe in God'
questions.explanation: An atheist is someone who disbelieves or lacks belief in the existence of God or gods.

questions.question: Select the correct indirect speech: He said, 'I have been waiting here for two hours.'
options: He said that he has been waiting there for two hours.
He said that he had been waiting there for two hours.
He said that he had been waiting here for two hours.
He said that he was waiting there for two hours.
questions.correctAnswer: He said that he had been waiting there for two hours.
questions.explanation: 'Have been waiting' changes to 'had been waiting' and 'here' changes to 'there'.

questions.question: Complete the analogy: 'Odometer : Mileage :: Compass : ?'
questions.explanation: An odometer measures mileage, just as a compass indicates direction.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate word: 'The committee _______ divided in their opinions.'
questions.explanation: When a collective noun refers to the individuals in the group acting separately, it takes a plural verb.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences uses a correct idiom?
options: He hit the nail on the hand.
He hit the nail on the head.
He hit the hammer on the head.
He hit the nail on the thumb.
questions.correctAnswer: He hit the nail on the head.
questions.explanation: 'Hit the nail on the head' means to describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem.

questions.question: Replace the phrase in bold with a better alternative if needed: 'She requested to the manager to consider her application.'
questions.explanation: The verb 'request' is transitive and is followed directly by the object without the preposition 'to'.

questions.question: Choose the correct order to form a meaningful sentence: P: to the cinema Q: last night R: we went S: to watch a movie
questions.explanation: 'We went to the cinema to watch a movie last night.' or 'We went to watch a movie to the cinema last night.' RPSQ is better: We went (R) to the cinema (P) to watch a movie (S) last night (Q).
